content.claim: Students from higher socioeconomic backgrounds are significantly more likely to attend early-selective academic tracks even when prior test scores are controlled for, and this selection correlates with diverging mathematical and reading performance between tracks.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Multiple studies converge but use different methodologies and definitions of selection
Causal magnitude of SES influence on track placement remains contested across studies
NBER study notes that early tracking's effect on mean performance is 'less clear'
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
